reviews.review_text: Poor attitude and will waste your time, was looking for emergency accommodation for my brother with mild intellectual disability, when waiting to see if he was successful, it was a flat out not successful and they said they were not obliged to give feedback back when asked. If looking for emergency accommodation I would give this place a huge miss. Going by all the other reviews I can see why. I should have read them first. Plus I agree, not only were management rude, but so were some of the guests. Definitely a one star caravan park.
Nice

Terrible run down park, full of rats due to all the clutter! Booked and payed over the phone for 4 days but once we arrived, we could only bare to be there for 2 nights! ( no refund ). Photos online do not do represent real life

Look elsewhere if you’re looking for a powered caravan site
Alot of Gossip, drama and amenities need upgrades

Had a bit of a mix up tried to book online but only able to do over phone. I booked for 3 adults and 2 children. We were given adjoining rooms but when we got there it had a double bed in one room and 3 single beds in the other. When I went to ask where the other bed was or if they had a trundle or a cot I was told a flat out no. Pretty disappointed in the attitude as I definitely booked for 3 adults and 2 children so it was very uncomfortable sleeping arrangements.  The location was great for the sports stadium and the pool was great as was the water views

Not a friendly welcome at all! No smile or anything. Girl behind counter scowled and told me our van was probably too big for the site allocated to us (like that was our fault? They knew how big it was when we booked) Very poor lighting at night along the roadways, so have to take a torch when visiting the toilet. Absolutely no paper towel or hand dryers in the ladies amenities. And very rude signs on the back of toilet doors bossing everyone around "who wants to sit in your faeces? who wants to smell your poo? who wants to share your dna?" SERIOUSLY?? A bit over the top. Showers were the best part of our stay. We really wouldn't go back.

Stayed one night with the family, the location and amenities were good. However if you have a young family I would steer clear. Very rude and nasty people living in the park. Park manager also had this attitude. Wouldn’t bother.

★★★★☆
Honestly? I came expecting a dodgy scene straight out of a true crime podcast — but what I found was a surprisingly peaceful little spot that doesn’t deserve half the shade it’s been thrown. Sure, the name “River Gardens” sounds like it should have swans and harp music, but this place holds its own.

Let’s talk about the bathrooms — because, let’s be real, that’s usually where things go from “hidden gem” to “biohazard.” Not here. They were actually super clean, smelled like nothing (a win in itself), and even had toilet paper — which in some places is rarer than a Lotto win.

The vibe? A little quiet, a bit under-the-radar, and yeah, you might see one or two characters that look like they’re mid side-quest — but no one bothered me. In fact, it was kind of peaceful. It’s the kind of place you’d bring a coffee, stare at the river for way too long, and pretend your life is together.

It’s not the Botanical Gardens — don’t expect manicured hedges and koi fish. But for a chill riverside breather with a clean loo and zero drama, River Gardens of Nerang is holding it down. Just don’t go flashing your valuables or feeding ibises

Absolutely Disgraceful Treatment!

My client, an NDIS participant, is now in a detrimental position and urgently needing stable accommodation. She previously lived at River Gardens Caravan Park for over a year and a half, always paid her rent on time, was never blacklisted, and kept entirely to herself with her cat. Upon departing, she was assured by management that she would always be welcome back.

However, after reapplying, because she is on a pension and options are extremely limited, she was given five different stories, leading to deep confusion and unnecessary emotional distress. Now, she has been told she is no longer welcome - without any explanation whatsoever.

The handling of this matter has been absolutely disgraceful. Management has shown extreme rudeness and a complete lack of professionalism or compassion. There has been zero clear communication throughout the process. This is especially unacceptable given my client’s vulnerable position and the assurance she was previously given.

The park environment is far from suitable -commonly known for ongoing drug and alcohol issues, and certainly not a safe or supportive place for those requiring stability. Not only that, but the caravan park is dirty, caravans are extremely old and rundown.
While Angie was kind and helpful, the experience overall- particularly with a woman named Sue - has left my client’s mental health in a worse state than ever before.

I would never recommend River Gardens Caravan Park to anyone. They should be held accountable for how they treat people, particularly those with support needs!!

Good experience with a helpful and friendly young man at reception who helped us out after complications with our booking. Other than that- it’s just a caravan park- does what it says on the tin. No rivers or gardens at river gardens caravan park lol. Lots of long term residents in cute little homes decorated with sweet plants. We just had a parking space for our car and rooftop tent at a staggering $50 per night, but honestly what was expected so close to surfers paradise. Toilets were clean and quiet.
Great place convenient and easy to access transport n major shopping arcades

If you wish to stay as a permanent stay clear. They lie and keep your money falsely and are nasty. the main woman there is dodgy to say the least.they tack on charges just for existing but wont answer when asked what the charges are for. the only time they bother answering is if you give them a bad review then they are quick in giving a response that again is a lie. If i could i would give minus stars.

Need advice want to river gardens caravan park with mums support dog who has a letter from the doctors stating she cannot leave mums side I ran the park manager sue only to get told that she can refuse the dog if she wants to even know we have a letter from the doctor and as you can see it goes against the commonwealth disability act if 1992. It states on park rules that she can be on site but not in the caravan I’m hitting my head against the brick wall after I spoke to her mid conversation she hung up on me not even wanting to listen to what I had to say either
Just as bad as mudgrbra caravan park in the old days in living conditions

Très jolie camping
Endroit calme

Disgusting behaviour of a caravan park
Threw a disability 18yr old girl out onto the streets when she had paid rent & was not in arrears..
Just because she has mental problems
DO NOT STAY HERE
PASS THIS INFO ONTO EVERYONE

Hi Everyone (including readers thinking of staying here at this incredible location)

My parents and I stayed in the wonderful, clean and most comfortable cabins numbered 1 & 2 right on the beautiful and peaceful river here at River Gardens in mid-to-late October 2023. We had a most enjoyable and delightful stay for our two nights away from where we live a few hours up the coast at Brisbane.

I was attending a seminar at a nearby hotel, so this location was perfect for me. The reason we chose here was because I was able to leave my Mum and Dad at the park for the day knowing that they were comfortable, safe and conveniently located to local shops. This even included a Woolworths store that is only a few minutes away from the park. My Dad still drives so perfect for us and especially having the complete kitchenette inside our cabins meant that Mum and Dad were able to look after themselves for the day. I was able to come home to a well prepared meal made by Mum and Dad for me in the room and we even had a dining table to sit and eat at. Wonderful facilities including well-appointed bathrooms and comfortable beds.

Over and above the quality of our accommodation, was the service that we received from the amazing Curtis and Drake. This father and son team were absolutely wonderful and they both went over and above what would normally already be 5-star service for the three of us during the two nights and two days of our stay. As well as being hospitable to the nth degree these two guys were so very friendly, courteous and respectful--and I must say they made our stay complete ;D.

We met other long-term tenants who had already been living here in the park for decades. One lovely couple we spoke to had already been happily living here at River Gardens for 40 odd years and they were even so kind as to go the local newsagent on the Sunday morning to buy us a newspaper. Lovely people everywhere!

All in all a wonderful experience for us as a family and now looking forward to going back again as soon as possible for another break away with no conferences to attend. Next time I am going to be swimming more in the beautiful pool right outside our doorstep and going for relaxing walks around the well-maintained grounds.

Thank you everyone for making our stay so very special <3.

Yours sincerely
The Bonds

Incorrect information provided and terrible customer service. The address is different on the website and only noticed when I got a text. Called up and staff (Tracey) was beyond rude repeatedly telling me I “need to pay more attention” also kept $30 of my booking even though I cancelled 2 weeks out. This is clearly an ongoing issue as they highlighted it in a text so why don’t you fix the google address instead of being rude and charging for a simple mistake….
Easy access with beautiful river views. Clear precise instructions.

More 4permanent living, hence nothe touristype facilities,  buthen nothe noise that goes with latter. Camp kitchen+pool however & if u dont need 2b on beach, a good alternative  NO WIFI & prices not lower than beachside, which could make commute annoying !

SO glad we got rejected for this place, really feel like we dodged a bullet. Don't rush into buying here just because you're really keen to get a place. We were all lined up to get a place here, was accepted to buy by the real estate but once I told them I require an assistance dog (I have a disability), we were told management rejected our application. I had an advocate try to talk with the manager Sue and tell her this is not okay, and not legal as it is disability discrimination. Long story short, the response was pretty much I don't HAVE to say why and you don't have proof, too bad. All in all, it was still difficult to find a place, but I'd much rather a disability friendly home and one that doesn't look like a halfway house at that XD
